description:
Replace the exact-match HashMap lookup in `SpecRegistry::resolve` (and `resolve_callee_sid`'s fallback) with a **subsumption-based search**: for a query `(fn_id, query_descrs)`, find the spec whose registered key is the **narrowest supertype** of `query_descrs` (element-wise: `query[i] ⊆ key[i]`). Deterministic tiebreak by SpecId.

## Why

Today `resolve_callee_sid` falls back to `callee.0` (the any-key SpecId) whenever exact-match lookup misses. This fires in real programs (fact_5, count_100k, recursive cases) because the typer registers narrow specs using its lub-aggregated FnTypes view while codegen-side resolve computes arg Descrs from the current per-spec FnTypes. The Descr representations for the same callsite can differ enough to miss hash-eq even when they represent the same type. The any-key has been silently absorbing this.

Subsumption-based dispatch makes representation differences irrelevant: as long as `query ⊆ key` for some registered spec, that spec is a safe dispatch target (its body was compiled assuming a wider domain than the query). The narrowest such key is the best match.

## Semantics

- `resolve(fn_id, query_descrs)` → Option<SpecId>
- Search criterion: for each registered `(key_descrs, sid)` under `fn_id`, accept iff `query_descrs[i].is_subtype(&key_descrs[i])` for all i.
- Among accepted candidates, pick the one whose key is **subtype-minimal** (narrowest). If multiple are subtype-incomparable, pick the lowest SpecId (deterministic).
- If no candidate accepts, return None. `resolve_callee_sid` then **errors loudly** (replaces today's silent `unwrap_or(callee.0)`).

## Invariant after this lands

The typer/codegen don't need byte-identical Descrs at the same callsite. They need their views to relate by subsumption: the typer registers a *covering set* of specs; codegen queries land safely on the narrowest cover. This is the wiggle room the previous architecture lacked.

## Out of scope (deferred — separate ticket)

**Best-match specialization quality.** Subsumption dispatch lands on the narrowest *registered* match, which may still be wider than ideal if the typer didn't register a tight-enough spec for that query. Example: typer registers `(fact, [int])` only; codegen queries `(fact, [int_lit_4])`; dispatch lands on `[int]` — correct but loses the literal-narrowing opportunity for that call. Strictly better than today (which dispatches to `[any]`), but not maximally specialized. Optimizing the typer to register tighter specs at every observable callsite is a separate concern — different ticket.

## Scope

- src/ir_codegen.rs SpecRegistry: replace HashMap exact-match `resolve` with subsumption search over per-FnId spec lists. Keep `register` API; restructure internal storage so subsumption search is reasonable.
- src/ir_codegen.rs `resolve_callee_sid`: drop `unwrap_or(callee.0)` fallback. On None, panic with a diagnostic (fn_id + query Descrs + registered specs) — the loud failure mode that .29.11 enforces.
- The any-key spec, where registered, is now just a particular (widest) supertype — no special-case handling in resolve.

## Tests

- Exact-match query still resolves to the same SpecId as today (regression).
- Narrower query than registered key dispatches to that wider spec (`int_lit_4 → [int]`).
- Multiple supertype candidates: picks the narrowest (e.g., `int_lit_4` against both `[int]` and `[any]` → picks `[int]`).
- Subtype-incomparable candidates: deterministic SpecId tiebreak.
- No covering spec registered: `resolve` returns None; `resolve_callee_sid` panics loudly.
- fact_5, count_100k, recursive fixtures stay green (regression).

## UNBLOCKS

- .29.10 (any-key on-demand): with subsumption dispatch, suppressing the any-key for purely-direct fns just means the *next-widest* registered narrow spec naturally takes over. No fallback gymnastics needed.

comments.text:
Two-tier lookup: try exact HashMap match first (the common case — typer and codegen often DO produce identical Descrs for the same callsite, and we shouldn't pay the subsumption cost when they do). On miss, fall back to subsumption search over the per-FnId spec list.

Structure:
- Keep `HashMap<(FnId, Vec<Descr>), SpecId>` as a fast-path hit cache.
- Add `per_fn: HashMap<FnId, Vec<SpecId>>` (or sorted-by-narrowness) for the subsumption fallback.
- `resolve` returns the HashMap hit if present; otherwise scans the per-fn list under subsumption.

This preserves today's O(1) cost on the hit path and only pays O(n_specs_for_fn) on the miss path — which is exactly the path the silent any-key fallback used to absorb.

title: Subtype-based callsite dispatch in spec_registry
